subtasks.description: Evaluate access control policies to verify the requestor has permission to update contract records for vendor 'TechFlow Solutions'
Update the contract record for 'TechFlow Solutions' in the contract management system to extend the term by 12 months
Send notification to the procurement team about the contract term extension for 'TechFlow Solutions'

plan.cost_rationale: This query explicitly requests a multi-step workflow with governance, data update, and notification actions. The plan uses three subtasks in sequence: (1) policy_action (small tier) to check update permissions—a straightforward access control check with one applicable rule; (2) multitool_plan (small tier) to execute the contract update using the enterprise tool registry; (3) multitool_plan (small tier) to invoke the notification tool. All three are small tier because the tasks are explicit, well-defined, and do not require complex reasoning. Dependencies are linear: permissions must be checked before update, and notification occurs after successful update.

output.reason: Cannot evaluate access control without requestor identity, role, and organization context. Request lacks: (1) user/requestor identification, (2) requestor's role/department, (3) resource ownership/sensitivity classification, (4) vendor relationship classification. Contract term extensions typically require documented approvals from procurement leadership and contract management authority. Policy evaluation impossible without upstream context.
applied_constraints: Require explicit user identity and role assertion
Require documented approval chain for contract modifications
Require audit trail for all contract record updates
Restrict notification to authorized procurement stakeholders only

context.note: Permission check failed - upstream policy compliance indicates deny_pending_approvals. Missing required context: user identity, role, organization, vendor classification, contract sensitivity level, and documented approval chain.

arguments.reason: Access control evaluation denied - insufficient context provided

arguments.message:
⚠️ Contract Term Extension Notification

Vendor: TechFlow Solutions
Action: Term extension of 12 months

Note: The upstream contract update was denied due to insufficient approval context. The following information is required before proceeding with the contract modification:

• User/Requestor identification
• Requestor role and department
• Vendor relationship classification
• Contract sensitivity level
• Documented approval chain
• Procurement leadership approval

Policies affected: POL-001, POL-002, POL-003, POL-004, POL-005

Please provide the missing context and resubmit for approval.

arguments.reason: Procurement team notified of contract extension request
